+{"id":"frankenredis-9itc","title":"fr-command: RESP3 map-typed replies emit as Array instead of Map (HGETALL, CONFIG GET, XINFO STREAM, CLUSTER SHARDS, CLIENT INFO, etc.)","description":"Multiple commands return map-typed replies in RESP3 mode upstream (Redis 7.2 networking.c::addReplyMap*) but fr's command implementations always emit RespFrame::Array regardless of session.resp_protocol_version. Affected commands include:

  - HGETALL h
  - CONFIG GET <param> (returns key/value map)
  - HRANDFIELD h N WITHVALUES
  - CLIENT INFO (single client info as RESP3 map)
  - XINFO STREAM/GROUPS/CONSUMERS

When redis-cli connects with -3, fr replies with array form (display: 'k\nv\nk\nv\n...'); upstream replies with map form (display: 'k v\nk v\n...').

Acceptance: when session.resp_protocol_version == 3, the listed commands emit RespFrame::Map(Some(pairs)) where each pair is (key, value). For fr-command this means routing the protocol_version through the dispatch path or providing a render-time override. fr-protocol already has the Map variant (crates/fr-protocol/src/lib.rs:13).

Reference: ad-hoc differential probe of fr (port 16700) vs vendored redis 7.2.4 (port 16701) with -3 flag.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T05:23:37.748476834Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T05:23:37.748476834Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id": "frankenredis-9jl4", "title": "v8s5 part a: document upstream DUMP/RESTORE payload format (fr-store reference)", "description": "Preparation sub-bead for br-frankenredis-v8s5. Extract the upstream DUMP payload layout from legacy_redis_code/redis/src/cluster.c::dumpCommand + rdb.c::rdbSaveObject + restoreCommand + rdb.c::rdbLoadObject and write it up as crates/fr-store/docs/dump-format.md.

Scope:
  * Overall payload: type_byte + rdbSaveObject + version (u16 LE) + CRC64 (u64 LE).
  * Version field semantics (currently upstream is version 11 for 7.2.x, 12 for 7.4.x).
  * Per-type rdbSaveObject layout for STRING/LIST_QUICKLIST_2/SET_INTSET/SET_LISTPACK/HASH_LISTPACK/HASH_METADATA/ZSET_LISTPACK/STREAM_LISTPACKS_3.
  * CRC64 polynomial (Redis CRC64 with the Jones polynomial, already implemented at crates/fr-persist/src/lib.rs:848).
  * A comparison table: current frankenredis-internal layout vs upstream expectation, field-by-field.

No code changes.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-24T00:17:59.107620059Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T16:24:22.963409003Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-24T16:24:22.961489188Z", "close_reason": "Reference doc crates/fr-store/docs/dump-format.md already exists (260 lines) covering envelope/footer, RDB length + string encodings, all upstream type tags (0/2/4/5/11/16/17/18/20/21 + Redis 7.4 24/25), per-type body layouts, fr-store delta vs upstream, and decoder/encoder checklist. No further work needed for the docs-only sub-bead.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}

 {"id": "frankenredis-fnf", "title": "Add XPENDING IDLE option support (Redis 6.2+)", "description": "## Problem

fr-command/src/lib.rs line 4629: XPENDING extended form parser requires exactly 6 or 7 args. Redis 6.2+ allows: XPENDING key group [IDLE min-idle-time] start end count [consumer]

With IDLE option: 8 args (no consumer) or 9 args (with consumer). Currently returns WrongArity.

## Implementation Plan

1. Check if argv[3] is \"IDLE\" (case-insensitive)
2. If IDLE present: parse min_idle_time from argv[4], shift start/end/count to argv[5..7], optional consumer at argv[8]
3. If IDLE not present: existing parsing at argv[3..5] for start/end/count, optional consumer at argv[6]
4. Pass min_idle_time to xpending_entries as a filter
5. In Store::xpending_entries, filter entries where idle_ms < min_idle_time

## Files to Modify

- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs: XPENDING argument parsing
- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs: xpending_entries add min_idle_time filter parameter", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-03-25T03:09:31.482460644Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-03-25T03:50:18.162252294Z", "closed_at": "2026-03-25T03:50:18.161172289Z", "close_reason": "XPENDING extended form now supports IDLE min-idle-time option (Redis 6.2+), with min_idle_ms filter parameter in xpending_entries", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "frankenredis-fnf", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-7o5", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-25T03:09:46.329057538Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}

+{"id":"frankenredis-fo1s","title":"fr-command/lua: EVAL error wording divergences for unknown command + script compile errors","description":"Several Lua-script error wording divergences from upstream Redis 7.2 script_lua.c:

- EVAL 'return redis.call("NOPE")' 0:
    fr 'ERR Error running script: ERR unknown command NOPE, with args beginning with: '
    redis 'ERR Unknown Redis command called from script script: <sha1>, on @user_script:1.'
- EVAL 'return redis.pcall("NOPE")' 0 (in-table form):
    fr 'ERR unknown command NOPE...'
    redis 'ERR Unknown Redis command called from script'
- EVAL '@@@' 0 (script compile error):
    fr 'ERR Error running script: unexpected character ...'
    redis 'ERR Error compiling script (new function): user_script:1: unexpected symbol near ...'

Upstream uses a fixed prefix 'Error compiling script (new function):' for compile errors and 'Unknown Redis command called from script' for unknown-command paths, plus appends script SHA1 + source location for the redis.call (not pcall) variant.

All cosmetic but visible.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T05:24:52.206990542Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T05:24:52.206990542Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id": "frankenredis-fppa", "title": "EPIC: Eliminate all stubs, placeholders, and hardcoded zeros from production code", "description": "# Stub/Placeholder Remediation Epic

## Motivation
A comprehensive audit identified 28+ stubs, placeholders, hardcoded zeros, and incomplete implementations across the FrankenRedis codebase. While many are in fr-command (the dispatch_argv path used by Lua scripts and AOF replay), some affect the runtime directly (ACL SAVE/LOAD, LATENCY tracking). Eliminating these is critical for:

1. **Lua script compatibility**: redis.call() from Lua goes through dispatch_argv, hitting fr-command stubs instead of runtime implementations for SLOWLOG, CLIENT, etc.
2. **Monitoring tool compatibility**: INFO stats with hardcoded zeros break Redis monitoring dashboards (redis-stat, RedisInsight, Grafana).
3. **Drop-in replacement fidelity**: The project's #1 goal is full behavioral parity with legacy Redis.

## Architecture Approach
Rather than duplicating runtime logic into fr-command, we use a **shared state pattern**:
- Add tracking counters and data structures to Store (which both fr-command and fr-runtime can access)
- For runtime-only state (blocked clients, maxmemory), pass context through new Store fields that the server populates each tick
- For LATENCY, add a LatencyTracker to Store that records samples from both dispatch paths

## Execution Layers (in dependency order)
1. **Foundation**: Add stat counters and tracking structures to Store
2. **LATENCY**: Build latency sample infrastructure + wire into command execution
3. **ACL persistence**: Implement real ACL SAVE/LOAD file I/O
4. **INFO accuracy**: Wire real counters into all INFO sections
5. **Stream metrics**: Track XINFO radix-tree and deletion metadata
6. **RDB LZF**: Implement LZF decompression for full RDB compatibility
7. **DEBUG commands**: Implement real behavior for DEBUG subcommands
8. **Lua-path stubs**: Forward SLOWLOG/CLIENT from fr-command to shared state

## Success Criteria
- Zero hardcoded zeros in INFO output where real data is available
- LATENCY LATEST/HISTORY returns real samples after threshold crossings
- ACL SAVE/LOAD round-trips to disk
- redis.call("SLOWLOG", "GET") from Lua returns real slow log entries
- RDB files with LZF-compressed strings can be decoded
- All existing conformance tests continue to pass
- New conformance tests validate each fix", "status": "closed", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "epic", "created_at": "2026-04-03T00:17:08.249471318Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-06T00:10:52.338586137Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-06T00:10:52.338382662Z", "close_reason": "done", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "frankenredis-fppa", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-01ht", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-06T00:05:05.652229373Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "frankenredis-fppa", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-4ai0", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-03T00:21:35.593914082Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "frankenredis-fppa", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-5h0q", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-03T00:21:35.720301180Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "frankenredis-fppa", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-8j5j", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-06T00:08:33.392765648Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "frankenredis-fppa", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-a5yy", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-05T23:47:45.192512961Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "frankenredis-fppa", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-bqot", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-06T00:01:29.947691624Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "frankenredis-fppa", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-klvg", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-03T00:21:35.858374458Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}

 {"id": "frankenredis-mmg", "title": "Implement SHUTDOWN graceful server termination", "description": "## Problem

fr-command/src/lib.rs line 10729: SHUTDOWN command returns OK but does not actually shut down the server. The comment says: \"Stub -- in production this would trigger graceful shutdown\".

## What Redis Does

SHUTDOWN [NOSAVE | SAVE] [NOW] [FORCE] [ABORT]:
1. Optionally triggers a final SAVE (RDB snapshot)
2. Flushes AOF if configured
3. Closes all client connections gracefully
4. Exits the process

SHUTDOWN ABORT cancels a pending shutdown.
SHUTDOWN NOSAVE skips the final snapshot.

## Implementation Plan

Since SHUTDOWN is a runtime-special command, it needs to signal the server event loop to begin graceful termination:

1. Add a shutdown_requested flag to ServerState
2. SHUTDOWN handler sets the flag (and optionally triggers SAVE)
3. fr-server main loop checks the flag each tick
4. On shutdown: flush writes, close connections, drop listener, exit

## Files to Modify

- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs: ServerState flag, SHUTDOWN handler logic
- crates/fr-server/src/main.rs: Check flag in main loop, graceful exit", "status": "closed", "priority": 3, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-03-25T03:10:45.912067485Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-03-25T03:45:05.661284398Z", "closed_at": "2026-03-25T03:45:05.660779813Z", "close_reason": "SHUTDOWN: sets shutdown_requested flag, server event loop exits gracefully with optional final SAVE", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
 {"id": "frankenredis-mmiv", "title": "Reject CONFIG SET io-threads as immutable", "description": "Redis marks io-threads as DEBUG_CONFIG | IMMUTABLE_CONFIG in legacy_redis_code/redis/src/config.c. FrankenRedis exposes io-threads as a static CONFIG parameter and currently accepts CONFIG SET through the static fallback. -{"id": "frankenredis-moer", "title": "fr-store: ZRANGEBYLEX/ZLEXCOUNT/ZRANGESTORE BYLEX accept unprefixed bounds that upstream rejects", "description": "Upstream Redis 7.2 t_zset.c::zslParseLexRangeItem requires lex bounds to start with '[' (inclusive), '(' (exclusive), or be exactly '-' (min) or '+' (max). Plain strings like 'a' or 'c' are rejected with 'ERR min or max not valid string range item'.

fr-store::zrangebylex / zrevrangebylex / zlexcount accept any string and treat them as inclusive bounds. As a result:
  - 'ZRANGEBYLEX z a c' returns the matching members (fr) vs error (upstream)
  - 'ZRANGESTORE z2 z 0 -1 BYLEX' returns 0 (fr) vs error (upstream)
  - 'ZLEXCOUNT z a c' returns the count (fr) vs error (upstream)

Acceptance: ZRANGEBYLEX / ZRANGESTORE-BYLEX / ZLEXCOUNT / ZREVRANGEBYLEX with bare-string bounds (no [/( prefix and not '-'/'+') return 'ERR min or max not valid string range item' on fr too.

Reference: ad-hoc differential probe of fr (port 16700) vs vendored redis 7.2.4 (port 16701).", "status": "closed", "priority": 3, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-30T05:15:28.125080586Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-30T05:21:15.917917Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "assignee": "cc_1", "closed_at": "2026-04-30T05:21:15.917917Z", "close_reason": "ZRANGEBYLEX/ZREVRANGEBYLEX/ZLEXCOUNT/ZRANGESTORE-BYLEX now validate lex bound prefix ([ ( - +) at the command layer and emit upstream's 'min or max not valid string range item' error otherwise. Verified byte-for-byte against vendored redis 7.2.4."}

Plain strings like 'a' or 'c' are rejected with 'ERR min or max not valid string range item'.

fr-store::zrangebylex / zrevrangebylex / zlexcount accept any string and treat them as inclusive bounds. As a result:

+{"id": "frankenredis-uanq", "title": "fr-store: GETRANGE with fully-negative range returns empty instead of clamped slice", "description": "fr-store::getrange at crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs:2290 only clamps the START offset to 0 after the negative-offset normalization, leaving END negative when both inputs are negative AND large in magnitude. The 's > e' guard then wrongly returns empty. Upstream t_string.c::getrangeCommand clamps both.

Repro:
  SET k 'Xbcdef' (len 6)
  GETRANGE k -100 -90:
    fr returns ''
    upstream returns 'X' (clamped to [0..0])

Fix: clamp  to 0 the same way  is clamped.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-30T05:34:06.261869666Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-30T05:34:18.738468Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "assignee": "cc_1", "closed_at": "2026-04-30T05:34:18.738468Z", "close_reason": "GETRANGE with fully-negative offsets now clamps both start and end to 0, returning the upstream-aligned clamped slice instead of empty. Verified across 10 negative-offset edge cases against vendored redis 7.2.4. Shipped in f69d42e."}

 {"id":"frankenredis-uczv","title":"fr-command: small zset error wording divergences (ZRANGEBYSCORE BAD-BOUND, ZUNIONSTORE WEIGHTS BAD, ZINTERCARD LIMIT BAD)","description":"Three small wording divergences from upstream Redis 7.2:
  - ZRANGEBYSCORE BAD-BOUND 5: fr 'value is not a valid float', redis 'min or max is not a float'
  - ZUNIONSTORE dst 1 z WEIGHTS BAD: fr 'value is not an integer or out of range', redis 'weight value is not a float'
  - ZINTERCARD 1 z LIMIT -1 / BAD: fr 'value is not an integer or out of range', redis 'LIMIT can't be negative'

All cosmetic but clients that match on error wording will trip. Fix at the parser: replace the generic float/int error with the upstream wording for these specific contexts.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T05:15:28.415938266Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T05:15:28.415938266Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-ud94","title":"fr-command: NOGROUP error wording differs from upstream (XPENDING/XAUTOCLAIM)","description":"XPENDING / XAUTOCLAIM emit 'NOGROUP No such consumer group <g> for key name <k>' when the consumer group is missing. Upstream Redis 7.2 emits 'NOGROUP No such key <k> or consumer group <g>' (note the 'No such KEY ... or consumer group' wording, used for both missing-key and missing-group cases — see legacy_redis_code/redis/src/t_stream.c).

Acceptance: error text byte-equal to upstream. Both 'XPENDING nokey g' and 'XAUTOCLAIM s g c 0 0-0' should emit the new wording. Reference: ad-hoc differential probe of fr (port 16700) vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 (port 16701). Fix: search-replace the old error wording in fr-command (likely a const).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T03:19:08.273775918Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T03:19:08.273775918Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
